info.description:
Laboratory Information System (LIS) REST API for CPRT.

## Authentication
All endpoints except `/health` and `/api/auth/login` require a **Bearer JWT**.
After logging in, copy the `token` value and click **Authorize** above, then enter:
`Bearer <your-token>`

## Typical workflow
1. **Login** → `POST /api/auth/login` → get token
2. **Register patient** → `POST /api/patients/register` → get patient_id
3. **Generate bill** → `POST /api/billing/new` (use service_ids from `GET /api/billing/services`)
4. **Create order** → `POST /api/orders`
5. **Sample collection** → `PATCH /api/lab/sample-collection/{billId}`
6. **Verify results** → `POST /api/lab/results/verify`
7. **Certify report** → `PATCH /api/lab/results/certify/{billId}`
8. **Get report** → `GET /api/lab/reports/{billId}`
info.title: CPRT LIS API

post.description:
Authenticate with login_id (or username) + password.
Copy the returned token and pass it as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` in every secured request.

parameters.description: Login credentials — use login_id or username interchangeably
parameters.name: body

200.description: Single-element array — token, user info and role permissions

400.description: Bad request — missing or malformed JSON body

post.description: Creates a bill record directly. Prefer POST /api/billing/new for the full workflow with services, discount and tax.
Appends a service/test line item to the bill specified by :id.

Creates a bill with one or more services, applies discount and tax, and records the received amount.
patient_id must be the UUID returned by POST /api/patients/register.
service_id values come from GET /api/billing/services.
The response includes bill_no, net amount, balance, and payment_status (Paid/Pending/Partial).

201.description: Single-element array with full bill summary

400.description: patient_id is required or services cannot be empty

get.description:
Returns all active services with their ID, name, rate and department.
Use the service_id values when generating a bill via POST /api/billing/new.

patch.description:
Records a payment received against an existing bill and recalculates balance and payment_status.
payment_status will be: Paid (balance=0), Partial (balance>0), or Pending (received=0).

200.description: Single-element array with updated payment summary

get.description:
Returns the complete lab report for a certified bill, including patient info, results, flags and certifier.
Only available after the bill has been certified via PATCH /api/lab/results/certify/:billId.

200.description: Single-element array with the full report

patch.description:
Marks the bill as certified by a doctor, making the report ready for dispatch.
step 3 (final) of the lab workflow. Must be called after VerifyResults.

200.description: Single-element array confirming certification and dispatch readiness

400.description: invalid bill id or certified_by missing

post.description:
Accepts an array of test parameter results (value + abnormal flag) and marks the bill as verified.
step 2 of the lab workflow. Each params entry needs param_id and result_value at minimum.

400.description: bill_id required, params empty, or verified_by missing

patch.description:
Records that the sample for the given bill has been collected. Generates a sample ID and worksheet linkage.
step 1 of the lab workflow: Billing → Sample Collection → Verify → Certify → Report.

400.description: invalid bill id, or sample_no / collected_by missing

post.description:
Creates a lab workflow order linked to an existing bill and patient.
Typically called right after a bill is generated to trigger sample collection.

patch.description:
Updates the workflow status of the specified order.
Valid statuses: Pending → InProgress → Completed.

get.description:
Full-text search across patient name, phone, and OP/IP no. Returns matching PatientSearchResult records.
Pass the search term as ?query= or ?search= — both are accepted.

200.description: Matched patients (empty array if none found)

post.description: Identical to POST /api/patients/register. Kept for backward compatibility.

201.description: Single-element array with the created patient record

post.description:
Creates a new patient record and returns the assigned patient_id (UUID) and patient_no.
age_unit accepts: Yrs, Mon, Days (and common aliases like years, months, days).

201.description: Single-element array with the created patient record

400.description: Validation error — e.g. first_name is required, invalid age_unit

get.description: Returns an array of PatientHistoryItem records for the given patient UUID.

patch.description: Partially updates a patient record. Only the fields supplied in the body are changed.

200.description: Single-element array with the updated patient profile

get.description: Returns an array of PatientHistoryItem records (one per service/test on each bill) for the given patient.

200.description: History entries — bill date, service, status and rate

get.description: Returns every user account. Requires SUPER_ADMIN or ADMIN role.

403.description: Insufficient role — need SUPER_ADMIN or ADMIN

patch.description:
Partially updates a user account. Requires SUPER_ADMIN or ADMIN role.
Send only the fields you want to change. All fields are optional.
If both password and password_hash are supplied, password takes precedence (server will hash it).

parameters.description: User ID (raw DB value, not the USR- prefixed one)
parameters.name: userId

400.description: invalid id, invalid payload, or invalid status value

get.description: Returns {"status":"ok"} when the server is up and reachable.

certified_by.description: Login ID or name of the certifying doctor — required

patient_id.description: Internal numeric patient ID (from patients table)

bill_id.description: Numeric bill ID returned by POST /api/billing/new (bill_no field)

op_ip_no.description: OP/IP registration number from the hospital

phone.description: Alias for phone_no — either field is accepted

discount_amt.description: Flat discount amount in currency units

services.description: At least one service is required

login_id.description: Use either login_id or username — both are accepted

permissions.description:
Permissions granted to this role
Possible values: PATIENT_READ, PATIENT_WRITE, BILLING_READ, BILLING_WRITE,
                 LAB_VERIFY, LAB_CERTIFY, USER_MANAGE

role.description: Role is one of: SUPER_ADMIN, ADMIN, DOCTOR, TECHNICIAN

collected_by.description: Login ID or name of the staff who collected the sample — required

sample_no.description: Unique sample bar-code / tube number — required

password.description: Plain-text password — will be bcrypt-hashed server-side

password_hash.description: Pre-hashed password — use only if you manage hashing yourself

account_group.description: Account group name e.g. Doctor, Admin, Technician

verified_by.description: Login ID or name of the verifying staff — required
